TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF ESTATE OF WILLIAM SYKES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FAIL TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437(b)(1) AND RULE OF COURT 3.1350(d) IN THAT PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SET FORTH THE ELEMENTS OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE UNDISPUTED FACTS RELATED THERETO. THE MOTION FAILS TO NEGATE THE EFFECT OF THE DEED CHANGING TITLE TO COMMUNITY PROPERTY, THOMASHA MAYFIELD'S PAYMENTS OF TAXES AND OCCUPANCY OF THE PREMISES FOR SIX YEARS, OR THE NOTARY'S RIGHT TO RELY UPON A VALID CALIFORNIA DRIVER'S LICENSE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE COMPLIANCE WITH THE RENT ORDINANCE IN SEEKING TO RECOVER RENT. THE OBJECTIONS OF THOMASHA MAYFIELD AND FREMONT FOR LACK OF AUTHENTICATION OF DOCUMENTS AND THE FIFTH AMENDMENT ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DEWITT DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED.
